message:
Sync .beads/issues.jsonl with latest issue state

Mechanical `br sync --flush-only` export: the JSONL ledger is the
on-disk representation of the beads SQLite DB and is refreshed so
other agents / git history can see the current state of the issue
graph (status, close reasons, updated timestamps).

No code change; this commit just keeps the JSONL store in lockstep
with the database so subsequent `br` operations on other checkouts
(and future agents) start from an up-to-date graph.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-1sip4","title":"[HIGH] ffs-tui lifetime syntax warning keeps workspace clippy red","description":"# Review finding

Workspace gate `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` stops in `ffs-tui` on a warning promoted to error.

## Failure
- File: `crates/ffs-tui/src/lib.rs:474`
- Item: `fn build_system_health_text(snap: &DashboardSnapshot) -> Text`
- Lint: `mismatched-lifetime-syntaxes`
- Suggested fix from compiler: return `Text<'_>` instead of bare `Text`

## Why this is high severity
This single signature keeps the whole workspace clippy gate red under `-D warnings`, so no one can currently use the mandated workspace lint gate as a merge signal.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

## Expected fix direction
Apply the explicit lifetime to the `Text` return type and rerun workspace clippy to ensure there are no additional red-gate warnings behind it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:49:06.552487169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:21.407827923Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:21.405205814Z","close_reason":"added explicit lifetime annotation to build_system_health_text return type","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","ffs-tui","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

## GOAL
Increase test coverage for the two weakest modules in ffs-repair:
- storage.rs: 5 tests / 974 LOC (1:195 ratio)
- recovery.rs: 6 tests / 915 LOC (1:153 ratio)

## TEST TARGETS

### storage.rs
- Dual-slot descriptor switchover, torn write recovery, generation monotonicity
- Raw vs packed symbol modes, CRC validation round-trips
- Layout validation for edge-case block group sizes

### recovery.rs
- Partial recovery, full corruption, noop recovery
- Evidence record emission, post-repair verification failure

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- storage.rs: ≥12 tests (from 5), recovery.rs: ≥12 tests (from 6)

-{"id":"bd-3g31b","title":"add mounted-path ext4 EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS coverage for COMPR enable and rejection paths","description":"Survey finding: `OpenFs::set_inode_flags` has real ext4 `EXT4_COMPR_FL` semantics in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs`: it rejects COMPR when the COMPRESSION incompat feature is absent, rejects enabling it on non-regular or non-empty files, and seeds the e2compr defaults when the feature is present. The mounted-path `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS` E2E currently proves only generic user-flag roundtrips, so this operator-visible branch remains unproven through FUSE.

Scope:
- Add a writable ext4 mounted-path scenario that enables `EXT4_COMPR_FL` on an empty regular file in an image with the COMPRESSION incompat bit set.
- Prove that a subsequent write/read roundtrip takes the expected e2compr path without regressing existing file data or flag masking.
- Add a mounted-path rejection scenario for at least one invalid COMPR enable case (missing feature bit or non-empty file) with deterministic errno / message assertions.
- Update parity notes if the public `SETFLAGS` contract now includes e2compr-specific behavior.

Acceptance:
- Mounted-path `SETFLAGS` coverage reaches the COMPR-specific success path and at least one rejection path.
- Tests prove the flag transition and post-ioctl file behavior, not only the raw ioctl return value.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:29:19.182951077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:54:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:54:00.000000000Z","closed_reason":"completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-mk94k","title":"[CRITICAL] FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX ignores caller out_size for v2 replies","description":"# Review finding

Recent fscrypt `_EX` parity work still leaves a dangerous output-size hole in the FUSE ioctl handler.

## Problem
In `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1658-1679`, `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX` only rejects buffers smaller than `header + v1_policy_size`. After that check, it always allocates and returns `header + actual_policy_size` bytes.

That means a caller can advertise an `out_size` large enough for a v1 policy but still too small for a v2 policy, and the handler will return the full v2 payload anyway.

## Evidence
- Handler: `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1658-1679`
- Existing negative test only covers `out_size < header + v1_policy_size`: `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:5165-5180`
- Existing v2 positive test only covers the full-size happy path: `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:5117-5162`

## Why this is critical
This is an ioctl boundary contract violation. The handler is supposed to respect the caller-advertised output capacity, but the current logic can emit more bytes than requested for v2 policies. Even if the kernel/FUSE layer truncates or rejects later, FrankenFS is still violating the protocol boundary in its userspace reply construction.

## Expected fix direction
- Reject or clamp when `out_size < header + actual_policy_size`
- Add a regression test for a v2 policy with `out_size` between the v1 and v2 minimums
- Confirm expected errno/behavior against the intended fscrypt `_EX` contract","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:51:04.915618280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:40:11.885371658Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:40:11.883483239Z","close_reason":"fixed ioctl handler to check actual policy size against out_size, added regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","ffs-fuse","fscrypt","ioctl","review"]}

 {"id":"bd-pzc6","title":"Implement i_version increment-on-write for NFSv4 and statx(2) compatibility","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 inodes have a 64-bit version counter split across two fields: i_version (l_i_version at offset 0x24, low 32 bits) and i_version_hi (offset 0x98, high 32 bits). This counter is used by NFSv4 for cache coherence (the NFS change attribute) and by statx(2) via STATX_CHANGE_COOKIE. The kernel increments it on every inode metadata or data change via ext4_inode_set_iversion_raw().

CURRENT STATE: Commit 4a1fb8a added the version and version_hi fields to Ext4Inode and wired them into parse/serialize, so the values are preserved across read-modify-write cycles. However, we never INCREMENT them — every write-back produces the same version counter the inode had when we read it.

IMPACT: NFS clients mounting a FrankenFS-served ext4 image via NFS will never see version changes, causing them to serve stale cached data indefinitely. The statx(2) STATX_CHANGE_COOKIE field will also be stale.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. Add a helper function bump_inode_version(inode: &mut Ext4Inode) that increments the 64-bit version counter (version + version_hi as a combined u64, then split back).
2. Call bump_inode_version in every write path that modifies the inode: ext4_write, ext4_create, ext4_mkdir, ext4_unlink, ext4_rmdir, ext4_rename, ext4_link, ext4_symlink, ext4_setattr (truncate, chmod, chown, utimes), ext4_setxattr, ext4_removexattr.
3. Only bump when metadata_csum or 64bit features are enabled (the field is only meaningful with extended inodes).
4. Add a test that performs a write, reads back the inode, and verifies version > original_version.
5. Add a test that performs multiple writes and verifies the version is monotonically increasing.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Every inode-modifying operation bumps i_version.
- Version is monotonically increasing within a mount session.
- Round-trip test: write, read-back, verify version increased.
- No version bump on read-only operations (getattr, read, readdir).
- The 64-bit counter wraps correctly at u64::MAX (wrapping_add).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:38.971610895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:23.987199529Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:23.986685286Z","close_reason":"Added bump_inode_version() called from touch_mtime_ctime() and touch_ctime(). All 3,591 tests pass. Commit f3c69f2.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","inode","nfs"]}

-{"id":"bd-ymg8v","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_readdir_image fixture leaves are missing tree_block_csum stamping","description":"# Review finding

`build_btrfs_readdir_image()` builds synthetic root-tree and fs-tree leaves for the readdir suite, but it never stamps `tree_block_csum` on either leaf.

## Evidence
- Builder: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23165-23311`
- It writes the root-tree leaf at `root_tree_logical = 0x4000` and the fs-tree leaf at `fs_tree_logical = 0x20000`.
- Unlike the fsops fixtures, this builder never invokes the existing checksum helper (`stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c`), and it switches to `nodesize = 16384`, so both synthetic tree blocks must be stamped consistently with the parser’s current checksum expectations.
- The workspace rerun still fails all readdir-specific consumers with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`:
  - `walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
  - `btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
  - `btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
  - `btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
  - `btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
  - `btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`

## Why this is high severity
This is a second stale synthetic-fixture family independent of the minimal open/root-tree fixture. It blocks all of the new btrfs directory-iteration regression coverage.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Stamp checksums for both generated leaves after all items/payloads are finalized, with nodesize-aware coverage for the 16 KiB fs-tree case.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:05:03.818965727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","close_reason":"Already shipped; builder already stamps both blocks and targeted btrfs_readdir repros now pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
